response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, excellent OOP principles, and proven TDD/agile expertise, he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience ended a decade ago (National Instruments 2014-2015), and his career has decisively pivoted toward web/Node.js/TypeScript development. The role demands current C++ mastery, multi-threaded systems programming, industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA), cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), and IoT/embedded systems experience—none of which appear in his recent work. He lacks industrial automation domain knowledge, message-broker architecture experience, and any evidence of secure development lifecycle or cybersecurity focus. Hiring would require an extensive ramp-up across multiple critical competencies, with significant execution risk.
strengths: Exceptional software craftsmanship foundation with deep TDD, CI/CD, and agile expertise demonstrated across multiple senior roles
Strong architectural thinking and OOP design patterns knowledge, evidenced by building distributed microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing ability through workshop design, code dojos, and cross-team collaboration across diverse tech stacks
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated (last substantial work: 2014-2015 at National Instruments, 10+ years ago); career has decisively moved to web/Node.js/TypeScript
Zero demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, message brokers (RabbitMQ, Kafka), MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA)
No evidence of secure development practices, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, SDL implementation, or application security risk mitigation—critical for this role
